data.description:
## Why
The per-child plan-review (log plum-strut-reek) raised major findings that are empirical: (S4) OpenGrep pre-validation must be engine-faithful + per-backend invalid-detector semantics unproven for ast-grep/scc-lizard; (S6) the conformance corpus omits the collision/member false-refute hazard and the yield is measured on a self-authored fixture; (S3) the deps lane is unvalidated. De-risk these before remediating.

## What
- E1 engine failure-mode matrix: does semgrep/opengrep --validate catch a bad rule WITHOUT scanning (engine-faithful pre-validation)? ast-grep malformed-rule behavior? scc/lizard (metric tools, no rule schema) -> invalid-detector n/a?
- E2 collision/member false-refute: does NAIVE ctags name-existence false-refute a common-name collision and a member/dotted ref? does a GUARDED rule (refute only bare, unambiguous, non-member names; else abstain) restore 0 false-refute?
- E3 independent-corpus yield: run ctags over rebar's OWN src and measure real refutation yield (not a self-planted fixture).
- E4 deps-lane existence: real registry/deps.dev probe on real + hallucinated + import-vs-dist + stdlib names -> refute/abstain/false-refute.
- E5 evidence normalization: map a real semgrep SARIF result + rebar_envelope -> the normalized three-valued evidence record (defines evidence-mapping concretely).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each experiment produces numbers + a clear de-risk verdict; reproducible artifacts committed under docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/.
- [ ] Results feed concrete ticket remediations (kind/dimension vocab, evidence-mapping, engine-faithful validation, per-backend semantics, collision/member guard, deps-lane + independent-corpus eval).
data.title: SPIKE 2: de-risk per-child review findings — engine failure-mode matrix, collision/member false-refute, independent-corpus yield, deps-lane existence, evidence normalization
